title: Nepeta rotundifolia
intro: Nepeta rotundifolia, also known as round-leaved catmint, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to soothe digestive issues, alleviate anxiety and insomnia, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities. The bioactive constituents of Nepeta rotundifolia include nepetalactone, a unique iridoid glycoside responsible for its sedative and antispasmodic effects, as well as other volatile oils and flavonoids. Nepeta rotundifolia is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Nepeta rotundifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This classification is based on its shared characteristics and genetic similarities with other plants in the same family. The Lamiaceae family consists of over 7,000 species, including popular herbs like mint, basil, and rosemary. Nepeta rotundifolia is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. Its classification within the Lamiaceae family is a result of its distinctive features, such as its square stem and oppositely arranged leaves.
uses: Nepeta rotundifolia, a species of catmint, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. It is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe digestive issues and wounds. The plant is also used to treat colds, fever, and respiratory problems due to its expectorant properties. In addition, Nepeta rotundifolia is believed to have antiseptic and antiviral properties, making it useful for treating infections. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Nepeta rotundifolia for medicinal purposes.

constituents: Nepeta rotundifolia contains a variety of bioactive compounds, including essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The essential oils in Nepeta rotundifolia are primarily composed of ketones, such as nepetalactone, which is responsible for its distinct fragrance and insecticidal properties. Nepetalactone has also been shown to possess sedative and anxiolytic effects in animals. Additionally, Nepeta rotundifolia contains flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, which have been reported to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds contribute to the plant's medicinal and pharmacological applications.

side_effects: Nepeta rotundifolia, also known as Round-leaved Catmint, is not commonly associated with severe side effects due to its limited documentation and traditional use. However, as with any plant, it may cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals. Ingesting large quantities of Nepeta rotundifolia may lead to gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and diarrhea.
